<a name="Port"></a>Port numbers for the various transports.

```go
const (
    // Port is the default port for DNS
    Port = "53"
    // TLSPort is the default port for DNS-over-TLS.
    TLSPort = "853"
    // QUICPort is the default port for DNS-over-QUIC.
    QUICPort = "853"
    // GRPCPort is the default port for DNS-over-gRPC.
    GRPCPort = "443"
    // HTTPSPort is the default port for DNS-over-HTTPS.
    HTTPSPort = "443"
)
```

# up

```go
import "github.com/coredns/coredns/plugin/pkg/up"
```

Package up is used to run a function for some duration. If a new function is added while a previous run is still ongoing, nothing new will be executed.

## Index

- [type Func](<#Func>)
- [type Probe](<#Probe>)
  - [func New\(\) \*Probe](<#New>)
  - [func \(p \*Probe\) Do\(f Func\)](<#Probe.Do>)
  - [func \(p \*Probe\) Start\(interval time.Duration\)](<#Probe.Start>)
  - [func \(p \*Probe\) Stop\(\)](<#Probe.Stop>)


<a name="Func"></a>
## type [Func](<https://github.com/coredns/coredns/blob/master/plugin/pkg/up/up.go#L26>)

Func is used to determine if a target is alive. If so this function must return nil.

```go
type Func func() error
```

<a name="Probe"></a>
## type [Probe](<https://github.com/coredns/coredns/blob/master/plugin/pkg/up/up.go#L19-L23>)

Probe is used to run a single Func until it returns true \(indicating a target is healthy\). If an Func is already in progress no new one will be added, i.e. there is always a maximum of 1 checks in flight.

There is a tradeoff to be made in figuring out quickly that an upstream is healthy and not doing much work \(sending queries\) to find that out. Having some kind of exp. backoff here won't help much, because you don't want to backoff too much. You then also need random queries to be performed every so often to quickly detect a working upstream. In the end we just send a query every 0.5 second to check the upstream. This hopefully strikes a balance between getting information about the upstream state quickly and not doing too much work. Note that 0.5s is still an eternity in DNS, so we may actually want to shorten it.

```go
type Probe struct {
    sync.Mutex
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="New"></a>
### func [New](<https://github.com/coredns/coredns/blob/master/plugin/pkg/up/up.go#L29>)

```go
func New() *Probe
```

New returns a pointer to an initialized Probe.

<a name="Probe.Do"></a>
### func \(\*Probe\) [Do](<https://github.com/coredns/coredns/blob/master/plugin/pkg/up/up.go#L32>)

```go
func (p *Probe) Do(f Func)
```

Do will probe target, if a probe is already in progress this is a noop.

<a name="Probe.Start"></a>
### func \(\*Probe\) [Start](<https://github.com/coredns/coredns/blob/master/plugin/pkg/up/up.go#L73>)

```

Start will initialize the probe manager, after which probes can be initiated with Do.

# upstream

import "github.com/coredns/coredns/plugin/pkg/upstream"
```

Package upstream abstracts a upstream lookups so that plugins can handle them in an unified way.

## Index

- [type Upstream](<#Upstream>)
  - [func New\(\) \*Upstream](<#New>)
  - [func \(u \*Upstream\) Lookup\(ctx context.Context, state request.Request, name string, typ uint16\) \(\*dns.Msg, error\)](<#Upstream.Lookup>)


<a name="Upstream"></a>
## type [Upstream](<https://github.com/coredns/coredns/blob/master/plugin/pkg/upstream/upstream.go#L16>)

Upstream is used to resolve CNAME or other external targets via CoreDNS itself.

```go
type Upstream struct{}
```

<a name="New"></a>
### func [New](<https://github.com/coredns/coredns/blob/master/plugin/pkg/upstream/upstream.go#L19>)

```go
func New() *Upstream
```

New creates a new Upstream to resolve names using the coredns process.

<a name="Upstream.Lookup"></a>
### func \(\*Upstream\) [Lookup](<https://github.com/coredns/coredns/blob/master/plugin/pkg/upstream/upstream.go#L24>)

```go
func (u *Upstream) Lookup(ctx context.Context, state request.Request, name string, typ uint16) (*dns.Msg, error)
```

Lookup routes lookups to our selves to make it follow the plugin chain \*again\*, but with a \(possibly\) new query. As we are doing the query against ourselves again, there is no actual new hop, as such RFC 6891 does not apply and we need the EDNS0 option present in the \*original\* query to be present here too.
